title: On a Chebyshev-Type Inequality for Sums of Independent Random Variables
why_relevant: This is the original Samuels paper on extremal probabilities for sums of independent nonnegative random variables with prescribed expectations. After scaling, the problem is a two-point special case of this small-deviation/extremal-tail framework.

title: The Markov Inequality for Sums of Independent Random Variables
why_relevant: Develops sharp Markov-type inequalities for sums of independent nonnegative variables, a natural framework for bounding probabilities of weighted Bernoulli sums crossing their mean.

title: Probability Inequalities for Sums of Bounded Random Variables
why_relevant: Hoeffding's classic paper gives comparison and concentration tools for sums of independent bounded variables. Its convex-order and extremal-distribution techniques are standard for reducing weighted Bernoulli problems to simpler extremal cases.

title: On Sums of Independent Random Variables with Unbounded Variance and Estimating the Average Degree in a Graph
why_relevant: Feige proves universal lower bounds for small deviations of nonnegative independent sums and formulates a conjecture closely related to Samuels' conjecture. The proof techniques are directly relevant after rewriting the weighted Bernoulli sum as a nonnegative independent sum at a threshold.

title: On some conjectures of Samuels and Feige
why_relevant: Studies weighted sums of independent nonnegative variables and the relationship between Samuels' and Feige's conjectures. The notation and extremal reductions closely match the scaled form of the present problem.

title: Small Deviations of Sums of Independent Random Variables
why_relevant: Improves Feige-type small-deviation bounds using moment methods and discusses Samuels' conjecture and two-point/Bernoulli subclasses. The Bernoulli-specialized arguments are likely useful for optimizing over the weights.

title: Nonnegative k-sums, fractional covers, and probability of small deviations
why_relevant: Connects small-deviation probabilities for independent nonnegative sums with weighted subset and fractional-cover arguments. The random-subset viewpoint is very close to interpreting the problem as the p-biased measure of a weighted threshold family.

title: Large matchings in uniform hypergraphs and the conjectures of Erdős and Samuels
why_relevant: Uses special cases of Samuels' conjecture to prove fractional matching results, and contains useful extremal formulations for independent nonnegative variables with two-point extremizers. These techniques may help handle weighted Bernoulli threshold events via hypergraph/fractional-cover reductions.

title: An improved bound for the Manickam–Miklós–Singhi conjecture
why_relevant: The Manickam–Miklós–Singhi line of work is the fixed-size-subset analogue of asking when random subsets have above-average weight. Conditioning the Bernoulli subset on its size suggests these combinatorial nonnegative-sum tools as a possible route.

title: Tight Lower Bound on the Probability of a Binomial Exceeding its Expectation
why_relevant: The equal-weight case reduces exactly to binomial tails at or just above the mean. This paper gives sharp lower bounds and extremal behavior for such binomial tail probabilities.

title: An Elementary Analysis of the Probability That a Binomial Random Variable Exceeds Its Expectation
why_relevant: Provides elementary, explicit estimates for binomial probabilities above the expectation. These estimates are useful both for equal-weight counterexamples and for approximating many-small-weight regimes.

title: On the probability that a binomial variable is at most its mean
why_relevant: Analyzes binomial distribution functions at the mean and formulates the Chvátal-Janson conjecture about the case p=m/n. This is directly relevant to the discontinuities that occur in the equal-weight version of the problem.

title: On the Chvatal-Janson conjecture
why_relevant: Proves the Chvátal-Janson conjecture for binomial variables with parameter m/n, giving precise control of tails exactly at integer means. This is important for determining which p fail already for equal weights.

title: On the Number of Successes in Independent Trials
why_relevant: Studies inequalities for sums of independent Bernoulli trials, including non-identical trials. These Poisson-binomial techniques are useful when reducing weighted Bernoulli sums to counts by discretizing or grouping weights.

title: Probabilistic Bounds on the Coefficients of Polynomials with Only Real Zeros
why_relevant: Poisson-binomial distributions have real-rooted generating polynomials, and this paper gives coefficient, tail, mode, and median estimates for such distributions. These tools can support reductions from weighted sums to Bernoulli-count distributions.
